issue.title:
Desktop: Fixes #14396: Fixed Google Docs paste producing stray ** and literal
in Markdown
issue.body:
fixes #14396 ### AI Assistance Disclosure AI tools were used to assist with: - Translating my designed logic into implementation code - Generating additional test case ideas for verification - Improving wording and clarity in the PR description The core logic, architectural decisions, and debugging process were done by me. All AI-generated output was carefully reviewed, validated, and modified where necessary. I fully understand the submitted code. ### Problem: When you paste content from Google Docs into the Rich Text editor, the resulting Markdown ends up with stray `** `markers, literal `
` tags, and ` `; characters scattered throughout the note. This happens because Google Docs puts some unusual HTML on the clipboard: - It wraps text in `` tags — bold tags that aren't actually bold - It leaves behind empty `` tags with no content - It wraps `
` inside styled `` tags (e.g.`
`) instead of using bare`
` - It uses `
` for line breaks instead of proper `

` paragraphs - It injects ` ` entities for spacing When our HTML-to-Markdown converter (Turndown) processes this, an empty `` becomes `**,` and the `
`tags cause broken paragraph spacing, and ` ` entities leak into the Markdown. ### Fix This PR adds a `sanitizeGoogleDocsHtml() `function in `resourceHandling.ts` that cleans up Google Docs HTML before it goes through the HTML→Markdown→HTML conversion. It only runs when the clipboard contains Google Docs content (detected by the` docs-internal-guid-` marker), so paste from other sources is completely unaffected. The cleanup does four things: 1. **Strips empty formatting tags** : removes `,` ``, ``, etc. that have no visible text 2. **Unwraps fake-bold wrappers** : replaces `content `with just content 3. **Splits `

` containing ` into separate `

` blocks**: first unwraps `
` from `` wrappers (Google Docs' actual format), then splits at `
` boundaries. Only targets`

` leaves `

  • `, ``, `
    ` alone 4. **Normalises top-level`
    ` into proper `

    ` blocks** : only at the top level; `
    ` inside lists, tables, and blockquotes is preserved, and existing`

    ` blocks are left alone Additionally, ` ` entities reintroduced by the `sanitizer's DOMParser`→`innerHTML` round-trip are cleaned to regular spaces. ### Architecture Safety - `sanitizeGoogleDocsHtml` is only called inside `processPastedHtml`, which is only used by TinyMCE (Rich Text editor) paste - CodeMirror (Markdown editor) does not call this function - No files in `packages/lib/`, `packages/app-mobile/`, `packages/app-cli/`, or `packages/server/` were modified - `HtmlToMd` and `MdToHtml` are untouched - The function is idempotent after sanitization, the `docs-internal-guid-` marker is removed, so a second pass returns immediately - Non-text content (``, `

    blocks ✓ should NOT normalize
    inside

  • ✓ should split

    containing
    into multiple

    blocks ✓ should handle trailing
    inside

    ✓ should unwrap
    from wrappers (real Google Docs format) ✓ should handle
    between and after

    blocks ✓ should handle Google Docs structure with empty paragraph breaks ✓ should NOT touch content when

    blocks already exist ✓ should return non-Google Docs HTML unchanged ✓ end-to-end: Google Docs paste produces clean Markdown with all content preserved Tests: 16 passed, 16 total ``` ### Demo https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6e208f14-b5a0-4792-82d0-053cae30e3f0

comments.body:
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough Adds a Google Docs HTML sanitizer and integrates it into the paste processing pipeline to clean docs-internal markup (empty inline tags, fake bold wrappers, and top-level breaks) before image handling and HTML→Markdown conversion. ## Changes |Cohort / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Google Docs HTML Sanitisation**
`packages/app-desktop/gui/NoteEditor/utils/resourceHandling.ts`|New exported `sanitizeGoogleDocsHtml(html: string)` implemented and invoked during pasted-HTML processing; removes empty inline formatting, unwraps faux bold wrappers, splits/normalises
-based paragraphs, and strips remaining top-level breaks.| |**Sanitiser Tests**
`packages/app-desktop/gui/NoteEditor/utils/resourceHandling.test.ts`|Added comprehensive tests for `sanitizeGoogleDocsHtml()` exercising empty element removal, wrapper unwrapping, paragraph splitting, list-item break preservation, trailing/empty-break handling, and integration with `processPastedHtml`.| ## Sequence Diagram(s) ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant User participant Clipboard participant processPastedHtml participant sanitizeGoogleDocsHtml participant ImageProcessor participant MarkdownConverter participant Editor User->>Clipboard: Copy from Google Docs User->>processPastedHtml: Paste into Rich Text Editor processPastedHtml->>sanitizeGoogleDocsHtml: Pass pasted HTML (detect docs-internal-guid-) sanitizeGoogleDocsHtml->>sanitizeGoogleDocsHtml: Remove empty inline tags / unwrap wrappers sanitizeGoogleDocsHtml->>sanitizeGoogleDocsHtml: Split/normalise
into

sanitizeGoogleDocsHtml->>processPastedHtml: Return sanitised HTML processPastedHtml->>ImageProcessor: Handle embedded images ImageProcessor->>MarkdownConverter: Forward processed HTML MarkdownConverter->>Editor: Provide converted Markdown / content ``` ## Suggested labels `editor`

tags in Markdown output. | | Description check | ✅ Passed | The description thoroughly explains the problem, the solution, architectural safety considerations, and includes test results and a demo, all directly related to the changeset. | | Linked Issues check | ✅ Passed | The PR fully addresses issue `#14396` by implementing sanitizeGoogleDocsHtml() to remove stray ** markers, literal
tags, and   entities from Google Docs pastes, directly resolving all reported problems. | | Out of Scope Changes check | ✅ Passed | All changes are within scope: modifications limited to resourceHandling.ts and its test file to add Google Docs sanitization; no unrelated changes to other packages or core conversion functions. |

# Transforming Code Reviews with AI

## Features

**Automated Reviews**: Continuous reviews of the pull requests including incremental commits. 

**Summarization**: Generates high-level summary and a technical walkthrough of the PR changes. 

**Line-by-line review**: Provides line-by-line suggestions committable with one click.

**Codebase verification**:  Verifies the impact on the overall codebase and identifies missing changes.

**Insights into your code**:  Ask any questions on your codebase within the pull request 

**Chat about your code** : Chat with the bot around your code. The more you chat, the smarter it gets.

**Issue Validation**:  Validates the PR against the linked issues and identifies other related issues

comments.body: I have read the CLA Document and I hereby sign the CLA

Your testing only covers the Rich text editor, but I think the place you have changed might impact the Markdown editor as well.

How does pasting text from Google Docs editor behave before and after the change?

Also, would it still work to paste raw html code into the Markdown editor, or would the new logic strip out the html source in that scenario?

EDIT: Ok it looks like processPastedHtml is only invoked by the Rich text editor, so testing for the markdown editor is out of scope
Please make sure you read our AI policy: https://github.com/joplin/gsoc/blob/master/ai_policy.md

@mrjo118 Thanks for pointing this out, after performing extensive testing with multiple types of Google Docs content including: simple paragraphs, mixed bold/italic text, soft line breaks, lists, and some edge cases with trailing breaks and spacing. During that testing, I did find one remaining issue. When Google Docs wraps everything inside a single`

`and inserts`
`between inline nodes, my current normalization logic doesn’t split that paragraph. Because of that, the `
` can still survive the HTML -> Markdown -> HTML roundtrip and show up as a literal `
` in Markdown. So the fix works for: - Empty inline formatting (no more stray **) - Fake bold wrappers - Non-breaking spaces - Top-level
sequences But it doesn’t yet fully handle `
` inside existing`

` blocks. I’ll update the implementation to safely split those paragraphs without touching lists, tables, or other structural elements. Also, I noticed a few potential edge cases flagged by the bots and earlier comments from @laurent22, so I’d like to address those as well. Once all of that is cleaned up, I’d prefer to do one more architectural pass to make sure: - The logic remains strictly scoped to Google Docs content - The Markdown editor flow is completely unaffected - We’re not over-normalizing anything unintentionally I’ll push an updated revision after tightening this up. Thanks again , the feedback definitely helped catch this gap. Could you advise on the preferred way to handle `
` inside existing `

` blocks while keeping the transformation safely scoped?

@laurent22 Thanks for pointing this out , I’ve now read the AI policy carefully.
I did use AI assistance during this contribution, mainly to help translate my designed logic into code form, generate additional test cases to verify behavior, and improve wording in the PR description.

The core logic and approach were designed by me, and I reviewed, tested, and verified all generated output before submitting. I fully understand the implementation.

I’ll update the PR to include a proper “AI Assistance Disclosure” section as required.

@mrjo118

I'm afraid this generated code is not reviewable because too large and complex, so I'm not confident something didn't get broken. I would suggest focussing on issues that you could potentially write yourself without AI assistance
